findings.title: Missing test for new `WalletEvent::TransactionsChainlocked` no-op arm
Identical `path_resolver` closure duplicated across both backends
TransactionsChainlocked no-op leaves is_chain_locked permanently stale for late-chainlocked transactions
Actual fee paid silently discarded

rounds.judgeSummary: Dependency upgrade is mechanical and correct. The one gap worth closing is the missing test for `TransactionsChainlocked` — every other `map_wallet_event` arm has one, and CLAUDE.md requires edge-case coverage. The discarded `_fee` and duplicated `path_resolver` are real but low-priority follow-up items, not blockers for a dep bump.

findings.title: TransactionsChainlocked no-op leaves per-transaction is_chain_locked stale
Actual fee paid logged at debug but not surfaced through the trait
Duplicate `path_resolver` closure across both backends

rounds.judgeSummary: Previous `_fee` discard addressed with `tracing::debug!` logging and a new test for the `TransactionsChainlocked` no-op arm. The chainlock staleness and fee-not-surfaced-to-UI concerns remain as pre-existing design gaps acknowledged in prior rounds — reasonable to defer to follow-up issues given this is a dependency upgrade PR.

findings.title: Asymmetric `BuilderError` → `BackendError` mapping between backends

rounds.judgeSummary: Findings 1, 4, and 5 are false positives — reviewers hallucinated a `build_signed().await` call that doesn't exist; both backends use synchronous `builder.build()`. Finding 2 references a `path_resolver` closure that also doesn't exist in the current code. The only real finding is the duplicated error-mapping logic (Finding 3), which is a repeat from prior rounds.

findings.title: New FFI raw pointer params lack null-guard reminder for future use
Duplicated `path_resolver` closure not extracted alongside `builder_error_to_backend`
Wallet write lock held across async `build_signed` await point

rounds.judgeSummary: Previous round findings (duplicate path_resolver, stale chainlock, discarded fee) all carried forward with no author replies. Three new findings this round: an FFI null-guard comment request, the recurring path_resolver duplication, and async lock holding. All are legitimate observations but none represent correctness bugs in the current code.
